audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Nicodemus, born from above/anew ambiguity, water and Spirit, Spirit/wind wordplay, lifted Son of Humanity, world/cosmos love, light/judgment, John witness about bridegroom and above/from earth contrast.

verses.restored: There was a person from the Pharisees, Nicodemus by name, a ruler of the Judeans.
This one came to him by night and said to him, Rabbi, we know that you have come from the Divine as teacher, for no one is able to do these signs you do unless the Divine is with him.
Jesus answered and said to him, Amen, amen, I say to you: unless someone is born from above/anew, one is not able to see the reign of the Divine.
Nicodemus says to him, How is a human able to be born when old? Is one able to enter a second time into the mother's womb and be born?
Jesus answered, Amen, amen, I say to you: unless someone is born of water and Spirit, one is not able to enter the reign of the Divine.
What has been born from ordinary human nature is ordinary human nature; what has been born from the Spirit is spirit.
Do not marvel that I said to you, You must be born from above/anew.
The wind/Spirit blows where it wills, and you hear its voice, but you do not know from where it comes or where it goes. So is everyone born from the Spirit.
Nicodemus answered and said to him, How are these things able to happen?
Jesus answered and said to him, You are the teacher of Israel, and you do not know these things?
Amen, amen, I say to you: we speak what we know and bear witness to what we have seen, and you do not receive our witness.
If I told you earthly things and you do not trust, how will you trust if I tell you heavenly things?
No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven, the Son of Humanity.
Just as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so it is necessary for the Son of Humanity to be lifted up,
so that everyone trusting in him may have age-long life.
For the Divine loved the world in this way: he gave the only Son, so that everyone trusting into him may not perish but may have age-long life.
For the Divine did not send the Son into the world to judge the world, but so that the world might be saved/healed through him.
The one trusting into him is not judged; the one not trusting has already been judged, because that one has not trusted into the name of the only Son of the Divine.
This is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and humans loved the darkness rather than the light, for their works were harmful.
For everyone practicing worthless/harmful things hates the light and does not come toward the light, so that one's works may not be exposed.
But the one doing the truth comes toward the light, so that one's works may be revealed, because they have been worked in the Divine.
After these things Jesus and his disciples came into the Judean land, and there he remained with them and baptized.
John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because much water was there; people were coming and being baptized.
For John had not yet been thrown into prison.
A discussion arose from John's disciples with a Judean about purification.
They came to John and said to him, Rabbi, the one who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have borne witness, look, this one is baptizing, and all are coming to him.
John answered and said, A human is not able to receive anything unless it has been given from heaven.
You yourselves bear me witness that I said, I am not the Messiah, but I have been sent before that one.
The one having the bride is the bridegroom; the friend of the bridegroom, standing and hearing him, rejoices with joy because of the bridegroom's voice. Therefore this joy of mine has been fulfilled.
That one must increase; I must decrease.
The one coming from above is above all. The one being from the earth is from the earth and speaks from the earth. The one coming from heaven is above all.
What he has seen and heard, this he bears witness to, and no one receives his witness.
The one receiving his witness has sealed that the Divine is true.
For the one whom the Divine sent speaks the words of the Divine, for he gives the Spirit without measure.
The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hand.
The one trusting into the Son has age-long life; the one refusing the Son will not see life, but the wrath of the Divine remains upon that one.
